intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.details: Registry P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) remains with the white rabbit anchor.

plant_audit.P1: Empty pot; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty pot; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf visible; appears stable but isolated.
plant_audit.P4: Empty pot; no biological activity detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, potentially over-saturated; no fungal blooms but high moisture retention.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the central black pot (P3/P4 area) acting as a calcium amendment or pest deterrent.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no debris or secondary growth.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a rapid decline of the primary specimens. The initial state showed a single green shoot in the black pot, which has remained largely static or slightly withered over the 5-day period. The yellow pot (P1) has remained consistently empty.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stagnant. The single leaf shows no signs of new growth or necrosis, suggesting a 'survival mode' or root-bound dormancy.
visual_health_inference.P1_P2_P4: Deceased/Lost. The lack of vegetative matter confirms total loss of the registered specimens.

anomalies: Persistent absence of P1 and P2 specimens throughout the observation window.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of severe depletion. Only one specimen (P3) remains viable, though it is not thriving. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively barren. The environment appears to be suffering from a lack of light or improper moisture management, leading to the loss of the majority of the registered inventory.
